content.rendered:
At the Grammy Awards in 2003, Bruce paid tribute to the recently departed Joe Strummer by teaming up with Steve Van Zandt, Elvis Costello and Dave Grohl to cover The Clash’s classic “London Calling.”

The original 1979 single and title track is arguably the band’s signature song. It’s also their most summational, capturing their sound, smarts, and eye on current events within a single track.

“London Calling” just barely missed the Top Ten in the UK, and it didn’t chart at all in America. But it grew in stature over the years, and in 2004 Rolling Stone ranked it #15 in their 500 Greatest Songs of All Time.

Bonus footage: Bruce and the E Street Band cover “London Calling” in concert at Hyde Park in 2009.

London Calling
First performed: February 23, 2003 (New York City, NY)
Last performed: October 29, 2009 (New York City, NY)

© April 9, 2018 / June 9, 2026

excerpt.rendered: Bruce led an all-star line-up at the 2003 Grammy Awards to pay tribute to the late, great Joe Strummer.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Brand New Cadillac
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce and The Wallflowers cover Vince Taylor's 1959 rockabilly classic, "Brand New Cadillac."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Gypsy Woman
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce's officially-released cover of Curtis Mayfield's "Gypsy Woman" adds a hint of danger to the mystery of the original. Take a listen and watch Bruce's only known performance inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Disgusted
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce joined Lucinda Williams and her band on stage in London to perform a cover of Melvin Jackson's "Disgusted."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Clampdown
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Late on the High Hopes Tour, Bruce added a Clash cover to his set lists–one that temporary E Streeter Tom Morello was more than a little familiar with.
